mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over three consecutive months compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS 3-month change vs 20-day forward return below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or average forward return in declining FEDFUNDS periods not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden surge in the unemployment rate above 5% signals the onset of a recession, raising default risk and lowering corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month increase above 1.5 percentage points and exceeding 5%
failure_condition: In at least 40% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5% with a 1.5pp jump, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive, or rank IC of UNRATE level vs forward return is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate a deep demand contraction that historically marks a recession trough; forward 60-day returns become positive as markets anticipate recovery.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month for two successive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day return is negative in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires, or rank IC of the joint decline signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) precipitates government stimulus and corporate cost-cutting, which sets the stage for a supply-side recovery; as the economy emerges from recession, forward 60-day equity returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 5% over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If in the 12 months following the trigger, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative on average, or rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change vs forward 60-day return is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 5% signals panic selling and extreme consumer pessimism, which is quickly mean-reverting as pent-up demand resurfaces, generating positive forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Average forward 5-day return is negative or zero in the 10 instances following such a decline, or the strategy generates a Sharpe ratio below -0.5 over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and the unemployment rate is elevated above 5%, the combined signal of accommodative monetary policy and labor slack indicates strong future economic recovery, boosting equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns in the joint condition are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the rank IC of a combined z-score is below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in consumer price inflation above 2.5% forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising the discount rate and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return in periods of CPI >2.5% y/y is positive, or if the rank IC of CPI acceleration vs forward return is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over two consecutive months signals healthy labor demand and rising consumer income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS monthly change vs 5-day forward return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average forward return in months with payroll growth is not positive.
